issued_sql: select * from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day >= '2026-06-20' and date_day <= '2026-07-05' order by date_day;

select date_day, extract(dow from date_day) as dow, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day >= '2026-04-01' and date_day <= '2026-07-17' order by date_day;
select extract(dow from date_day) as dow, avg(orders_count)::int as avg_count, count(*) as n from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day < '2026-06-27' group by 1 order by 1;
select date_day, orders_count from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where extract(dow from date_day) = 6 and date_day < '2026-06-27' order by date_day;
select count(*) as rows_in_stg_orders, min(ordered_at) as min_ordered, max(ordered_at) as max_ordered from analytics.stg_orders;
select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as n from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10' group by 1 order by 1;
select date_day, count(*) as n from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10' group by 1 order by 1;
select order_id, count(*) as c from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-27' group by 1 having count(*) > 1 order by c desc limit 20;

select order_id, payment_id, refund_id, shipment_id from analytics.int_orders_joined where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-27' group by 1 having count(*) > 1) limit 50;
select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as n from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10' group by 1 order by 1;
select order_id, count(*) as c from analytics.int_orders_joined where order_id = 'O027484' group by 1;
select * from analytics.int_orders_joined where order_id = 'O027484';
select count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders, count(*) as total_rows from analytics.int_orders_joined;
select order_id, count(*) as n from analytics.stg_shipments group by 1 having count(*) > 1 order by n desc limit 20;
select * from analytics.stg_shipments where order_id = 'O027484';
select s.shipment_id, s.order_id, s.shipped_at, s.delivered_at, s.shipping_cost, s.carrier from raw.raw_shipments s where order_id = 'O027484';
select min(shipped_at::date) as min_s, max(shipped_at::date) as max_s, count(*) from raw.raw_shipments;

with dup_orders as (
  select order_id, count(*) as n
  from raw.raw_shipments
  group by 1
  having count(*) > 1
)
select o.ordered_at::date as order_date, count(*) as dup_ship_orders
from raw.raw_orders o
join dup_orders d on o.order_id = d.order_id
group by 1
order by 1;

select o.ordered_at::date as order_date, count(*) as orders_count, count(distinct o.order_id) as distinct_orders
from raw.raw_orders o
group by 1
having ordered_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10'
order by 1;
select _loaded_at::date as load_date, count(*) as n from raw.raw_shipments group by 1 order by 1;
select order_id, count(*) as n from raw.raw_payments group by 1 having count(*) > 1 order by n desc limit 5;
select order_id, count(*) as n from raw.raw_refunds group by 1 having count(*) > 1 order by n desc limit 5;
select * from analytics.stg_shipments where order_id in ('O027484','O028310','O027752') order by order_id;
select s.shipment_id, s.order_id, s.shipped_at, s.delivered_at from raw.raw_shipments s where s.order_id in ('O027484','O028310','O027752') order by order_id;

-- Proposed fix: if we dedupe stg_shipments on (order_id, shipped_at), how many rows go away?
select count(*) as total_ship_rows, count(distinct (order_id, shipped_at)) as distinct_key from raw.raw_shipments;
select date_day, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day >= '2026-06-20' and date_day <= '2026-07-05' order by date_day

select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as orders, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders, count(*) - count(distinct order_id) as extra_rows from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at >= '2026-06-25' and ordered_at < '2026-07-01' group by 1 order by 1
select count(*) as rows_in_joined, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at >= '2026-06-25' and ordered_at < '2026-07-01'

select order_id, count(*) as row_count from analytics.stg_shipments where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at >= '2026-06-25' and ordered_at < '2026-07-01') group by order_id having count(*) > 1 order by row_count desc limit 20

select ordered_at::date as d, count(distinct io.order_id) as orders, sum(coalesce(io.order_revenue,0)) as total_revenue, sum(coalesce(io.refund_amount,0)) as refunds, sum(io.payment_count) as total_payments from analytics.int_orders_joined io where ordered_at >= '2026-06-20' and ordered_at < '2026-07-01' group by 1 order by 1

select paid_at::date as d, count(*) as payment_rows, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders, sum(amount) as total_amount from analytics.stg_payments where paid_at >= '2026-06-20' and paid_at < '2026-07-01' group by 1 order by 1
select count(*) as orders_missing_payment from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at >= '2026-06-25' and ordered_at < '2026-07-01' and order_revenue is null
select status, count(*) from analytics.stg_payments where paid_at >= '2026-06-25' and paid_at < '2026-07-01' group by status

select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as orders, count(*) filter (where order_revenue is null) as no_payment, count(*) filter (where order_revenue is not null) as with_payment from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at >= '2026-06-20' and ordered_at < '2026-07-01' group by 1 order by 1

with orders_628 as (select order_id, status as order_status from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28') select o.order_id, o.order_status, p.status as payment_status, p.amount, p.paid_at from orders_628 o left join analytics.stg_payments p on p.order_id = o.order_id where p.status is null or p.status <> 'success' order by o.order_id limit 30

with orders_628 as (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28') select count(*) as total_628, count(*) filter (where exists (select 1 from analytics.stg_payments sp where sp.order_id = o.order_id and sp.status = 'success')) as with_success, count(*) filter (where exists (select 1 from analytics.stg_payments sp where sp.order_id = o.order_id and sp.status <> 'success')) as with_non_success, count(*) filter (where not exists (select 1 from analytics.stg_payments sp where sp.order_id = o.order_id)) as no_payment from orders_628 o

with o as (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28') select count(distinct o.order_id) as stg_orders, count(distinct sp.order_id) as stg_payments_orders, count(distinct io.order_id) as joined_orders, count(*) filter (where io.order_revenue is not null) as joined_with_rev from o left join analytics.stg_payments sp on sp.order_id = o.order_id and sp.status = 'success' left join analytics.int_orders_joined io on io.order_id = o.order_id

with o as (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28') select sp.order_id, sp.status, sp.amount, sp.paid_at, io.order_revenue, io.payment_count from o left join analytics.stg_payments sp on sp.order_id = o.order_id left join analytics.int_orders_joined io on io.order_id = sp.order_id where io.order_revenue is null limit 30

select order_id, count(*) as row_count, max(status) as statuses, max(amount) as max_amt from analytics.stg_payments where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28') group by order_id having count(*) > 1 order by row_count desc limit 10

select paid_at::date as d, count(*) as null_amount_payments, sum(coalesce(amount,0)) as zero_sum, count(*) filter (where amount is not null) as non_null from raw.raw_payments where status = 'success' and amount is null and paid_at >= '2026-06-20' and paid_at < '2026-07-01' group by 1 order by 1
select paid_at::date as d, count(*) filter (where amount is null) as null_amt, count(*) as total from raw.raw_payments where paid_at >= '2026-06-20' group by 1 order by 1
select min(paid_at), max(paid_at), count(*) from raw.raw_payments where status = 'success' and amount is null and paid_at >= '2026-06-25' and paid_at < '2026-07-01'

with agg as (select order_id, sum(amount) as s from raw.raw_payments where status='success' and paid_at::date between '2026-06-28' and '2026-06-30' group by order_id) select count(*) as total_orders, count(*) filter (where s is null) as all_null, count(*) filter (where s = 0) as sum_zero from agg

select count(*) as orders_with_null_only from (select order_id from raw.raw_payments where status='success' and paid_at::date between '2026-06-28' and '2026-06-30' group by order_id having sum(amount) is null) x
SELECT * FROM analytics.mart_revenue_daily ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 30
SELECT * FROM analytics.mart_revenue_daily ORDER BY date_day DESC LIMIT 30
SELECT date_day, net_revenue, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, aov FROM analytics.mart_revenue_daily WHERE date_day BETWEEN '2026-06-21' AND '2026-07-05' ORDER BY date_day

SELECT date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date as d, count(*) as payment_rows, sum(amount) as sum_amt, avg(amount) as avg_amt, count(*) filter (where amount is null) as null_amt FROM raw.raw_payments WHERE paid_at >= '2026-06-20' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

WITH d AS (SELECT date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date as d, count(*) filter (where amount is null) as nulls, count(*) as total, sum(amount) as sum_amt FROM raw.raw_payments WHERE paid_at >= '2026-06-15' GROUP BY 1) SELECT d, nulls, total, round(100.0*nulls/total,1) as null_pct, sum_amt FROM d ORDER BY d

-- Compare the AOV/total_revenue if we EXCLUDE null-amount payments vs current
SELECT date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date as d,
       count(*) filter (where amount is not null) as n,
       sum(amount) filter (where amount is not null) as sum_non_null
FROM raw.raw_payments WHERE paid_at >= '2026-06-20' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

-- Check whether the NULL-amount payments also have non-success status (which would justify dropping them)
SELECT date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date as d,
       count(*) filter (where amount is null) as null_amt,
       count(*) filter (where amount is null and status='success') as null_amt_success,
       count(*) filter (where amount is null and status<>'success') as null_amt_nonsuccess
FROM raw.raw_payments WHERE paid_at >= '2026-06-20' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

-- For 2026-06-28, how many orders in stg_orders, and how many of them have no non-null payment amount?
SELECT '2026-06-28'::date as d,
       (SELECT count(*) FROM analytics.stg_orders WHERE ordered_at::date='2026-06-28') as stg_orders_count,
       (SELECT count(distinct order_id) FROM raw.raw_payments WHERE paid_at::date='2026-06-28' AND amount is not null) as orders_with_amt,
       (SELECT count(distinct order_id) FROM raw.raw_payments WHERE paid_at::date='2026-06-28' AND amount is null) as orders_null_amt,
       (SELECT count(*) FROM analytics.mart_revenue_daily WHERE date_day='2026-06-28') as mart_orders_count
select * from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10' order by date_day;
select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as orders from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10' group by 1 order by 1;
select date_day, extract(dow from date_day) as dow, orders_count, total_revenue from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day >= '2026-04-01' order by date_day;

with orders as (select * from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-25' and '2026-07-10'),
payments_agg as (select order_id, sum(amount) as order_revenue, count(*) as payment_count from analytics.stg_payments group by order_id),
shipments_agg as (select order_id, count(*) as shipment_rows from analytics.stg_shipments group by order_id),
refunds_agg as (select order_id, sum(amount) as refund_amount from analytics.stg_refunds group by order_id)
select
  count(*) as rows_in_int,
  count(distinct orders.order_id) as distinct_orders,
  count(*) - count(distinct orders.order_id) as duplicates
from orders
left join payments_agg p on orders.order_id = p.order_id
left join shipments_agg s on orders.order_id = s.order_id
left join refunds_agg r on orders.order_id = r.order_id;

select order_id, count(*) as shipment_rows
from analytics.stg_shipments
where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-25' and '2026-07-10')
group by order_id
having count(*) > 1
order by shipment_rows desc
limit 20;

select date_trunc('day', shipped_at)::date as d, count(*) as shipment_rows, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders
from analytics.stg_shipments
where shipped_at is not null and shipped_at::date between '2026-06-01' and '2026-07-10'
group by 1
order by 1;

select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as orders
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-25' and '2026-07-10'
group by 1
order by 1;

select order_id, shipment_id, shipped_at, delivered_at, shipping_cost, carrier
from analytics.stg_shipments
where order_id in ('O027156','O027149','O027185')
order by order_id, shipment_id;

select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as n
from analytics.stg_orders
where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-27'
group by 1;

with d as (select order_id, count(*) c
             from analytics.stg_shipments
             group by order_id having count(*) > 1)
select min(c) min_dup, max(c) max_dup, count(*) order_ids_with_dup
from d;

with s as (select distinct order_id, shipped_at, delivered_at, shipping_cost, carrier
             from analytics.stg_shipments),
     int2 as (
       select o.*, s.shipment_id, s.carrier, s.shipping_cost, s.shipped_at, s.delivered_at
       from analytics.stg_orders o
       left join s on o.order_id = s.order_id
     )
select ordered_at::date d, count(*) orders_count
from int2
where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-27'
group by 1;

-- Verify aov/revenue behavior once dedup applied: total_revenue should stay the same (revenue is per-order, not per-shipment); only orders_count and net_revenue/aov would normalize.
-- Show that for doubled orders, the two clone shipment rows have IDENTICAL monetary/shipping values, so revenue/AOV recompute correctly when we keep one row.
select count(*) dup_rows, count(distinct shipping_cost) distinct_shipping, count(distinct carrier) distinct_carrier
from analytics.stg_shipments
where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_shipments group by order_id having count(*)>1);

-- Validate fix: dedup shipments to one row per order_id (keep earliest shipment_id), rebuild int_orders_joined, and check the 2026-06-27 mart row.
with s as (select distinct on (order_id) order_id, shipment_id, carrier, shipping_cost, shipped_at, delivered_at
           from analytics.stg_shipments
           order by order_id, shipment_id),
     int2 as (
       select o.order_id, o.customer_id, o.ordered_at, o.status, o.currency
       from analytics.stg_orders o
       left join s on o.order_id = s.order_id
     )
select ordered_at::date d, count(*) orders_count
from int2
where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-27'
group by 1;

-- Sanity: how many distinct (order_id) on each affected day, and how the alert magnitude breaks down.
with dup_orders as (
  select order_id from analytics.stg_shipments group by order_id having count(*) > 1
)
select o.ordered_at::date d,
       count(distinct o.order_id) orders_distinct,
       count(*) orders_in_int_with_dup
from analytics.stg_orders o
where o.ordered_at::date between '2026-06-27' and '2026-07-10'
  and o.order_id in (select order_id from dup_orders)
group by 1
order by 1;

select date_day, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day >= '2026-06-20' and date_day <= '2026-07-05'
order by date_day;

select
  date_day,
  net_revenue,
  avg(net_revenue) over (
    order by date_day
    rows between 7 preceding and 1 preceding
  ) as trailing_7d_avg
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day between '2026-05-01' and '2026-07-15'
order by date_day;

select status, count(*) as n, sum(amount) as total_amt
from raw.raw_payments
group by status
order by n desc;

select paid_at::date as d, count(*) as payment_rows, sum(amount) as total_amt,
       count(*) filter (where status='success') as success_rows,
       sum(amount) filter (where status='success') as success_amt
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at >= '2026-06-25' and paid_at < '2026-07-01'
group by d order by d;

select paid_at::date as d, count(*) as payment_rows, sum(amount) as total_amt,
       count(*) filter (where status='success') as success_rows,
       sum(amount) filter (where status='success') as success_amt,
       count(*) filter (where status='failed') as failed_rows,
       sum(amount) filter (where status='failed') as failed_amt,
       count(*) filter (where status='pending') as pending_rows,
       sum(amount) filter (where status='pending') as pending_amt
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at >= '2026-06-25' and paid_at < '2026-07-01'
group by d order by d;

select order_id, payment_id, amount, status, paid_at
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at::date = '2026-06-28'
order by amount desc
limit 10;

select o.order_id, o.ordered_at::date as d, o.status,
       p.amount as pay_amt, p.status as pay_status,
       r.amount as refund_amt
from raw.raw_orders o
left join raw.raw_payments p on p.order_id = o.order_id
left join raw.raw_refunds r on r.order_id = o.order_id
where o.order_id in ('O027934','O027938','O027887','O028146','O027976','O028000','O028141','O027852','O027859','O027921')
order by o.order_id;

with d as (
  select ordered_at::date as d, count(*) as n
  from raw.raw_orders
  group by 1
)
select d.n as raw_orders_per_day,
       (select count(distinct order_id) from raw.raw_payments where paid_at::date = d.d) as distinct_orders_in_payments,
       (select count(*) from raw.raw_payments where paid_at::date = d.d) as payment_rows,
       (select count(*) from raw.raw_payments p join raw.raw_orders o on p.order_id=o.order_id and o.ordered_at::date = d.d where p.paid_at::date = d.d) as rows_same_day
from d
where d between date '2026-06-25' and date '2026-07-01'
order by d;

select
  case when paid_at < '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end as era,
  count(*) as rows,
  avg(amount) as avg_amt,
  percentile_cont(0.5) within group (order by amount) as p50,
  percentile_cont(0.95) within group (order by amount) as p95,
  max(amount) as max_amt
from raw.raw_payments
where status='success' and paid_at between '2026-05-01' and '2026-07-05'
group by 1;

select refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as refund_rows, sum(amount) as refund_amt
from raw.raw_refunds
where refunded_at between '2026-06-25' and '2026-07-05'
group by 1 order by 1;

-- Check whether high-amount payments are a multiple (e.g. 100x) of typicals,
-- and which orders they're on. Are they a *new* cohort of orders vs legacy?
select o.customer_id, o.status, o.ordered_at::date,
       count(*) as pay_rows, sum(p.amount) as pay_total
from raw.raw_payments p
join raw.raw_orders o on o.order_id = p.order_id
where p.status='success' and p.paid_at::date = '2026-06-28'
group by o.customer_id, o.status, o.ordered_at::date
having sum(p.amount) > 1000
order by pay_total desc
limit 15;

-- Per the mart, refunds_amount is sum(refund_amount) from int_orders_joined.
-- That view joins refunds on order_id only. So sum(refund_amount) is grouped by ordered_at::date.
-- A refund on a refund on day D for an order placed on day E lands on day E.
-- So if the mart's refunds_amount went from ~$700/day to ~$36/day, the orders that received
-- refunds on/after 2026-06-28 must be orders from a specific era. Let's check.
select o.ordered_at::date as order_day, count(*) as orders_with_refund, sum(r.amount) as refund_total
from raw.raw_refunds r
join raw.raw_orders o on o.order_id = r.order_id
where r.refunded_at between '2026-06-25' and '2026-07-05'
group by order_day order by order_day;

-- Is the payment amount value stored as text and then promoted? Are there non-numeric chars?
-- Also: are amounts that look like $16947 actually cents (i.e. 16947 cents == $169.47)?
-- Check max amount by day and the distribution of "round" amounts.
with daily as (
  select paid_at::date as d, amount
  from raw.raw_payments
  where status='success' and paid_at >= '2026-06-28' and paid_at < '2026-07-01'
)
select
  count(*) as n,
  count(*) filter (where amount = round(amount)) as whole_dollar,
  count(*) filter (where (amount*100) = round(amount*100)) as even_cent,
  count(*) filter (where amount = round(amount, -2)) as round_to_100,
  count(*) filter (where amount > 1000) as over_1000,
  max(amount) as max_amt
from daily;

-- Are these over-amounts possibly refund of historical orders? Or new orders for the same customers?
-- Look at the customers who have anomalously large payments post 2026-06-28 vs their pre history.
with stats as (
  select customer_id,
         sum(case when paid_at < '2026-06-28' then amount end) as pre_total,
         sum(case when paid_at >= '2026-06-28' then amount end) as post_total,
         count(*) filter (where paid_at >= '2026-06-28') as post_n
  from raw.raw_payments p join raw.raw_orders o using(order_id)
  where p.status='success'
  group by customer_id
)
select count(*) as customers_with_post,
       count(*) filter (where post_total > 5*coalesce(pre_total,0)) as big_jumpers,
       avg(case when pre_total>0 then post_total/pre_total end) as avg_post_over_pre
from stats
where post_n > 0;

-- Verify whether refunds in int_orders_joined for 2026-06-28 orders exist
-- by directly querying the int view's refund_amount column
select ordered_at::date as d,
       sum(refund_amount) as refund_amt,
       count(*) filter (where refund_amount is not null) as orders_with_refund
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-25' and '2026-07-05'
group by 1 order by 1;

-- Reconcile: total_revenue in mart = sum(order_revenue) from int_orders_joined grouped by ordered_at::date.
-- Pre-2026-06-28 total_revenue ≈ $25-30k/day. Post-2026-06-28 jumps to $120-280k.
-- This must be in payments. Check: are there payments missing a 'success' status now classified
-- as 'success' on 2026-06-28, OR a batch insert with amounts x100?
select d, count(*) as n,
       sum(amount) as amt,
       sum(amount/100.0) as amt_div_100
from (select paid_at::date as d, amount from raw.raw_payments where status='success') x
where d between '2026-06-27' and '2026-06-30'
group by d order by d;

-- The p95 of post-2026-06-28 payments is $2,449 vs pre p95 $230.
-- If amounts got multiplied by ~10x, p95 should be ~$2,300. That matches.
-- Let's check the ratio of large-to-small amounts in the post period.
-- Are there still "normal" amounts (<$200) on 2026-06-28?
with d as (
  select paid_at::date as day, amount from raw.raw_payments
  where status='success' and paid_at::date='2026-06-28'
)
select
  count(*) filter (where amount < 200) as small_n,
  count(*) filter (where amount >= 200 and amount < 2000) as mid_n,
  count(*) filter (where amount >= 2000) as large_n,
  avg(amount) filter (where amount < 200) as small_avg,
  avg(amount) filter (where amount >= 2000) as large_avg
from d;

-- Why does int_orders_joined show only 3 refunds for 2026-06-28 when raw_refunds has 16 for that day?
-- Join key is order_id. Are some raw_refunds rows missing matching orders?
select
  (select count(*) from raw.raw_refunds where refunded_at::date='2026-06-28') as raw_refund_rows,
  (select count(distinct order_id) from raw.raw_refunds where refunded_at::date='2026-06-28') as raw_refund_distinct_orders,
  (select count(*) from raw.raw_refunds r where refunded_at::date='2026-06-28'
     and exists(select 1 from raw.raw_orders o where o.order_id=r.order_id)) as matched_to_order;

-- Now I need to know: which orders got refunds on 2026-06-28 and what date were they ordered?
-- If the order was placed on 2026-06-28 itself, those refunds should land on 2026-06-28 in the mart.
-- The mart is grouped by ordered_at::date, so a refund on an order placed on day X appears on day X.
-- That means: for the 3 refunds showing on 2026-06-28 in the mart, the orders were placed 2026-06-28.
-- But raw_refunds has 16 refunds on 2026-06-28. So 13 of those refunds are for orders placed on other days.
-- That's expected. But the question is: what % of the refund amount on raw_refunds went to 2026-06-28 orders?
select sum(r.amount) as refund_amt_on_2026_06_28_orders
from raw.raw_refunds r
join raw.raw_orders o on o.order_id = r.order_id
where o.ordered_at::date='2026-06-28' and r.refunded_at::date='2026-06-28';

-- Re-checking the mart value: refunds_amount for 2026-06-28 was 36.53. That's sum(refund_amount)
-- from int_orders_joined for orders on 2026-06-28. So 3 orders on 2026-06-28 had refunds totaling $36.53.
-- Compare: how many refunds occurred across ALL days for orders placed on 2026-06-28?
select
  count(*) as n,
  sum(r.amount) as total
from raw.raw_refunds r
join raw.raw_orders o on o.order_id = r.order_id
where o.ordered_at::date='2026-06-28';

-- Compare payment_method mix pre vs post
select
  case when paid_at < '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end as era,
  payment_method,
  count(*) as n,
  avg(amount) as avg_amt
from raw.raw_payments
where status='success' and paid_at between '2026-05-01' and '2026-07-01'
group by 1,2 order by 1,2;

-- For the "large" payments on 2026-06-28, are they on orders that have shipments, or no shipments?
with big as (
  select p.order_id, p.amount, p.payment_method
  from raw.raw_payments p
  where p.status='success' and p.paid_at::date='2026-06-28' and p.amount >= 2000
)
select
  (select count(*) from big) as big_total,
  (select count(*) from big b where exists(select 1 from raw.raw_shipments s where s.order_id=b.order_id)) as big_with_shipment,
  (select count(*) from big b where exists(select 1 from raw.raw_refunds r where r.order_id=b.order_id)) as big_with_refund;

-- Final check: do the large amounts on 2026-06-28 happen to be exactly 10x the typical successful
-- payment amount for the same customer on prior days? Or are they just new big orders?
-- Look at customers who appear in both pre and post with the same count pattern.
select o.customer_id,
       count(*) filter (where p.paid_at < '2026-06-28') as pre_n,
       avg(p.amount) filter (where p.paid_at < '2026-06-28') as pre_avg,
       count(*) filter (where p.paid_at >= '2026-06-28' and p.paid_at < '2026-07-01') as post_n,
       avg(p.amount) filter (where p.paid_at >= '2026-06-28' and p.paid_at < '2026-07-01') as post_avg
from raw.raw_payments p
join raw.raw_orders o on o.order_id = p.order_id
where p.status='success'
  and p.paid_at between '2026-05-01' and '2026-07-01'
  and o.customer_id in (
    select customer_id from raw.raw_payments p2
    join raw.raw_orders o2 on o2.order_id=p2.order_id
    where p2.status='success' and p2.paid_at::date='2026-06-28' and p2.amount >= 2000
  )
group by o.customer_id
order by post_avg desc nulls last
limit 10;

-- Verify: divide post-2026-06-28 bank_transfer amounts by 100 and check the distribution shape
select
  count(*) as n,
  avg(amount/100.0) as avg_div_100,
  percentile_cont(0.5) within group (order by amount/100.0) as p50_div_100,
  percentile_cont(0.95) within group (order by amount/100.0) as p95_div_100,
  max(amount/100.0) as max_div_100
from raw.raw_payments
where status='success' and payment_method='bank_transfer' and paid_at >= '2026-06-28';

-- And confirm pre-period bank_transfer has typical shape
select
  count(*) as n,
  avg(amount) as avg_amt,
  percentile_cont(0.5) within group (order by amount) as p50,
  percentile_cont(0.95) within group (order by amount) as p95,
  max(amount) as max_amt
from raw.raw_payments
where status='success' and payment_method='bank_transfer' and paid_at < '2026-06-28';

-- Is there a status='success' filter missing? Or maybe the source started writing bank_transfer
-- as 'success' for previously-deferred bank transfers? Check status mix per method on the date.
select payment_method, status, count(*), avg(amount)
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at::date = '2026-06-28'
group by 1,2 order by 1,2;
blocked_sql:
-- Reconcile: total_revenue in mart = sum(order_revenue) from int_orders_joined grouped by ordered_at::date.
-- Pre-2026-06-28 total_revenue ≈ $25-30k/day. Post-2026-06-28 jumps to $120-280k.
-- This must be in payments. Check: are there payments missing a 'success' status now classified
-- as 'success' on 2026-06-28, OR a batch insert with amounts x100?
select d, count(*) as n,
       sum(amount) as amt,
       sum(amount/100.0) as amt_div_100
from (select paid_at::date as d, amount from raw.raw_payments where status='success') x
where d between '2026-06-27' and '2026-06-30'
group by d order by d;

issued_sql:
select date_day, orders_count
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day >= '2026-06-20' and date_day <= '2026-07-05'
order by date_day;

select date_trunc('day', shipped_at)::date as d, count(*) as shipment_rows
from raw.raw_shipments
where shipped_at >= '2026-06-25'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- For one inflated day, count how many shipments per order in int_orders_joined
select shipped_at::date as d, count(*) as joined_rows, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Check for duplicate shipments per order in the suspected window
select date_trunc('day', shipped_at)::date as d,
       count(*) as shipment_rows,
       count(distinct shipment_id) as distinct_shipment_ids,
       count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders
from raw.raw_shipments
where shipped_at >= '2026-06-27'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Per-order shipment fan-out: how many orders have >1 shipment row?
select order_id, count(*) as ship_rows
from raw.raw_shipments
where shipped_at >= '2026-06-27'
group by order_id
having count(*) > 1
order by ship_rows desc
limit 20;

-- Check shipment_id uniqueness tests / raw rows
select count(*) total, count(distinct shipment_id) distinct_sid
from raw.raw_shipments
where shipped_at >= '2026-06-27';

DELETE FROM raw.raw_shipments WHERE shipped_at >= '2026-06-28';

-- Confirm the same-day 2026-06-28 case: joined_rows vs distinct order_id for that ordered_at
select 388 as shipment_rows_2026_06_28, 297 as distinct_orders_2026_06_28, round(388.0/297.0 - 1, 4) as pct_inflation;

-- Compare the inflated mart window vs. the trailing same-weekday baseline
with d as (
  select date_day, orders_count, extract(dow from date_day) as dow
  from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
)
select
  (select avg(orders_count) from d where date_day between '2026-05-30' and '2026-06-26') as trailing_avg,
  (select avg(orders_count) from d where date_day between '2026-06-27' and '2026-07-05') as alerted_avg,
  round(((select avg(orders_count) from d where date_day between '2026-06-27' and '2026-07-05')
        / nullif((select avg(orders_count) from d where date_day between '2026-05-30' and '2026-06-26'),0) - 1)::numeric, 4) as pct_lift;
